free_text: Production of Li and B in proton and alpha-particle
reactions on 14N at low energies
Department of Physics
RIKEN Nuclear Data Group
On. Changed to lower case and 4-digit year.
EXP in SF9 deleted. FACILITY corrected.
On. STATUS moved
Evaporated adenine onto backing of aluminum or carbon
foils were used. The thicknesses of adenine were 1-2 mg
per square cm. The thicknesses of aluminum backing were
1.4 to 7 mg per square cm. The backing was served as
catcher foil. The adenine surfaces were coated with a
polystyrene film of about 0.1 mg per square cm thick.
The number of counts in the 14N elastic peak was
monitored by using a solid state detector. The cross
sections were determined within about 4% uncertainty.
No analysis of the energy error is given by the
authors.
The estimated uncertainty in the absolute
cross sections is 9% up to 18 MeV of
incident energy. Above 19 MeV, the absolute
uncertainty is approximately 20%. For
details see reference.
Scanned from Fig.1, p2136 in reference
On. IND in SF5 deleted. MONITOR corrected.
On. STATUS added
Evaporated adenine onto backing of aluminum or carbon
foils were used. The thicknesses of adenine were 1-2 mg
per square cm. The thicknesses of aluminum backing were
1.4 to 7 mg per square cm. The backing was served as
catcher foil. The adenine surfaces were coated with a
polystyrene film of about 0.1 mg per square cm thick.
The number of counts in the 14N elastic peak was
monitored by using a solid state detector. The cross
sections were determined within about 4% uncertainty.
GeLi was used to check the peak.
The error of the gamma ray intensity was taken as
0.0016. Compilers consider that the branching ratio to
the 478 KeV state mentioned by the authors corresponds
to the intensity of gamma ray emitted from the state.
No analysis of the energy error is given by the
authors.
The absolute uncertainty of each data is about 7.5%
which arises primarily from uncertainties of 5% in
determining the normalization factor of beam flux
multiplied by target thickness and 5% in the detector
efficiency determination.
Scanned from Fig.2, p2137 in reference
On. IND in SF5 deleted. MONITOR corrected.
On. STATUS added. DETECTOR corrected.
Continuum region of the reaction spectra
About 100 micro-gram per square centimeters adenine
was evaporated onto 25 micro-gram per square
centimeters carbon foils.
Two dimensional displays of mass vs particle
energy were obtained.
The estimated uncertainties by background subtraction
are 1 to 12%.
Scanned from Fig.4, p2139 in reference

On. STATUS added. Heading corrected.
About 100 micro-gram per square centimeters adenine
was evaporated onto 25 micro-gram per square
centimeters carbon foils.
Two dimensional displays of mass vs particle
energy were obtained.
The total cross sections were made by
integration of center of mass differential
cross sectional data by authors.
The estimated uncertainties by background subtraction
are 1 to 12%.
The uncertainties estimated by authors in the
differential cross section interpolation were from 5
to 15% of the total cross section.
The energy level of 12C is given.
